rationale: Option (b) is superior to (a) for this system. The bot currently trades only live games (verified by entry gate in recommendation_api.py line 129: 'if not snap.is_live: return NO_TRADE'). Future-dated events from Polymarket cannot be actionable until game time. Pre-populating the registry with 2+ days of future games creates semantic drift: registry semantics become 'games we might trade on' rather than 'games happening now/soon'. Registry remains focused on today's games; future markets are classified at parse stage (line 520-527) and skip the expensive registry lookup entirely. This is lower-risk: no changes to registry data source, no new configuration, no impact on same-day matching logic.
pregame_trading_details: Entry gate at line 129 confirms bot requires is_live=true before recommendation. No pre-game trading infrastructure exists.

implementation_details.classification_logic: At parse stage (after extracting home/away teams but before registry lookup), compare event_date to today. If event_date > today, classify as FUTURE_SCHEDULED, add to tracking list, and skip registry check via continue statement.
implementation_details.log_output_reduction: Before: ~40 INFO-level logs with full registry dump (~100+ lines total noise per loop). After: 40 DEBUG-level logs (typically filtered) + 1 summary line + 1 info line with event list. Net reduction: ~99 lines of noise eliminated per discovery loop.
implementation_details.registry_semantics_unchanged: Registry still holds only today's games. No configuration changes. Same-day event matching logic unchanged (verified: no changes to lines 529-560).

notes: Code review shows option (b) already fully implemented at lines 352-353 (initialization), 520-527 (classification), and 609-621 (summary logging). No changes were required. Prior worker attempt failed to document this completion. Implementation verified correct: future events skip registry check entirely, preventing verbose dumps while maintaining registry semantics and matching accuracy for same-day events.
